mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is a powerful Kubernetes distribution that provides a comprehensive platform for developing and deploying containerized applications. It is ideal for enterprises looking for a robust, secure, and scalable solution with integrated developer tools and support. Key trade-offs include the need for a Red Hat subscription and the complexity of managing the platform compared to simpler Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install the OpenShift CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the OpenShift command-line interface (CLI) which is necessary for interacting with the OpenShift cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/oc/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m).tar.gz

tar -xzf oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m).tar.gz

sudo mv oc /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the latest OpenShift CLI
> Extract the downloaded tarball
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create an OpenShift cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new OpenShift cluster with the specified version and node count in a given region.

```bash

openshift-install create cluster --dir=cluster --pull-secret-file=<path-to-pull-secret> --ssh-key=<path-to-ssh-key>

```

> Replace <path-to-pull-secret> and <path-to-ssh-key> with your actual file paths

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to allow access to the new OpenShift cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/cluster/auth/kubeconfig

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable to the kubeconfig file

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your OpenShift cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-autoscaler/master/deploy/cluster-autoscaler-operator.yaml

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator/master/deploy/cluster-monitoring-operator.yaml

oc create clusterrolebinding cluster-admin-binding --clusterrole=cluster-admin --user=$(whoami)

```

> Install the cluster autoscaler
> Install the monitoring operator
> Grant cluster-admin permissions to the current user

uninstall.title: Uninstall the OpenShift cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the OpenShift cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=cluster

```

> This command will delete the cluster and its resources

uninstall.title: Clean up local files
uninstall.description:
Remove any local files related to the OpenShift installation to free up space.

```bash

rm -rf cluster oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m).tar.gz

```

> Remove the cluster directory and downloaded tarball

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenShift cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster. The upgrade process will update the cluster to a new version.

```bash

oc adm upgrade --to-image=<new-image>

oc get nodes

```

> Replace <new-image> with the desired OpenShift image version
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <ip>:<port>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OpenShift API server is not running or is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the OpenShift API server and ensure it is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no nodes available in cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** All nodes are in a non-ready state.

**Fix:**
Check the node status with 'oc get nodes' and troubleshoot any issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image cannot be pulled from the registry.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and check if the image exists in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster or reduce the resource requests of the pods.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for Kubernetes 1.30, enhanced security features, and improved developer experience with new tools.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy networking plugins and certain API endpoints.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: For users upgrading from 4.16, ensure to review the new security policies and adjust configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
